title: tiliacora macrophylla
intro: Tiliacora macrophylla, also known as Chakramarda or Makoi, is a plant species belonging to the Menispermaceae family, native to tropical Africa and Asia. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities. The health benefits of Tiliacora macrophylla include its potential in treating various ailments such as fever, diarrhea, and skin infections, as well as its ability to reduce inflammation and improve wound healing. Tiliacora macrophylla can be prepared into various herbal formulations, including decoctions, infusions, and extracts, which are used in traditional medicine to treat a range of health conditions.
